title: Guest user with group-based admin escalation
narrative: A guest user is invited to a group that transitively holds a privileged Entra directory role. Because group-based role assignments inherit to every direct and nested member, the guest gains tenant-wide privilege the moment the invitation is accepted. Cross-tenant access policies that don't restrict what external users can do compound this.

steps.action: Accept an invitation for a guest user into the home tenant.
steps.gain: Guest foothold in the target tenant.

steps.action: Enumerate groups and discover one that holds User Administrator or similar via nested membership.
steps.gain: Privileged directory role by transitive inheritance.

steps.action: Create new users, reset passwords, or grant Application.ReadWrite.All to a controlled app.

blast_radius.max_privilege: Entra directory admin role (User Admin, Application Admin, etc.).

title: Legacy authentication bypass of Conditional Access
narrative: Tenant allows legacy authentication protocols (IMAP, POP, SMTP AUTH, older EWS) that do not support interactive MFA. Conditional Access policies built for modern auth do not apply to these flows. An attacker who obtains any password hash or sprays a weak password logs in with no MFA prompt.

steps.action: Password-spray known user principal names against the legacy auth endpoint.
steps.gain: Valid password for a real account.

steps.action: Authenticate via IMAP / SMTP AUTH which ignores the modern-auth Conditional Access policy.
steps.gain: Authenticated mailbox session without MFA challenge.

data_at_risk: OAuth consent grants granted from mailbox links
services_at_risk: Any app the user can consent to

title: PIM eligible role activated without approval gate
narrative: A high-privilege role (Global Admin, User Access Admin) is assigned Eligible via PIM but the activation policy requires neither an approver nor justification. Any identity that compromises the eligible principal's creds can self-activate to full admin with zero friction.

steps.action: Sign in as the eligible principal.
POST /activate to PIM with any excuse text; activation completes without approver review.
steps.gain: Active tenant-admin role for the full activation window.

blast_radius.initial_access: Stolen credentials of an eligible role-holder.
blast_radius.max_privilege: Whatever the eligible role grants — often Global Admin.

title: Consent phishing via pre-authorized multi-tenant app
narrative: User consent to third-party apps is allowed tenant-wide without admin approval. An attacker registers a multi-tenant app requesting Mail.Read and Files.Read.All and sends phishing links. A single click grants the attacker's app persistent OAuth tokens — far more durable than a stolen password because tokens survive password resets.

steps.action: Register a multi-tenant Entra app with privileged delegated scopes.
steps.gain: App artifact ready to request consent.

steps.action: Send phishing URL with /adminconsent or /authorize?prompt=consent.
Use the refresh token to read mail, OneDrive, Teams messages for months.
steps.gain: Long-lived data access that survives password reset.

blast_radius.max_privilege: Delegated scope — typically Mail.Read, Files.Read.All, User.Read.

title: Inactive privileged account with stale credentials
narrative: A user account with an admin role has not signed in for 180+ days and still has an active password credential. Unused admin accounts are a favorite target — nobody notices anomalous activity, MFA prompts go to a forgotten device, and credentials are often recycled from breach corpora.

steps.action: Match an inactive admin UPN against breached-credential dumps.
steps.gain: Credential pair for a dormant admin account.

steps.action: Authenticate; because the account is idle, no user notices the sign-in log.

blast_radius.max_privilege: Whatever role the dormant account holds.

title: Break-glass account without MFA or monitoring
narrative: A break-glass Global Admin is excluded from every Conditional Access policy by design — but it is also excluded from sign-in monitoring alerts. The account credentials live in a shared password manager. Any insider with manager access can sign in silently.

steps.action: Pull the break-glass password from the shared vault.
Sign in from anywhere; no CA policy blocks, no alert fires.

title: Service principal with privileged Graph role + no cred rotation
narrative: A service principal holds a dangerous Microsoft Graph application permission (Directory.ReadWrite.All, AppRoleAssignment.ReadWrite.All) and its client secret never expires. Any exposure of that secret — CI log leak, GitHub push, developer laptop exfil — is a permanent tenant backdoor.

steps.action: Grep GitHub / Postman / CI logs for the client secret.
Mint a Graph token and call directoryRoles to grant itself Global Admin.

title: Conditional Access gap on partner-tenant guest admin
narrative: A B2B guest admin from a partner tenant is granted directory roles in the home tenant, but the home Conditional Access policies only target the home UPN suffix. The partner's admin authenticates under their own tenant with its own MFA story, and the home tenant has no visibility into whether that MFA is strong.

steps.action: Compromise a partner admin account (weak MFA at partner side).
Cross-tenant access to home; CA doesn't apply.
steps.gain: Home tenant admin role via partner auth.

blast_radius.max_privilege: Whatever directory role the guest holds.
data_at_risk: Directory objects visible to guest role

title: Managed identity with excessive subscription-scope role
narrative: A resource's managed identity is assigned Contributor or higher at subscription scope. Any code execution on that resource (IMDS token) yields subscription-wide privilege. This is the bread-and-butter Azure privilege-escalation chain.

steps.action: curl the IMDS endpoint for a token scoped to management.azure.com.
steps.gain: ARM token for the managed identity.

steps.action: Enumerate role assignments; identity holds Contributor on /subscriptions/<id>.
Create new role assignment, new storage with anon access, exfiltrate VM disks.

title: Unmonitored directory role change
narrative: No Activity Log alert is configured for role assignment changes AND Entra audit logs are not streamed to a SIEM. An attacker who gains Global Admin (via any other chain) can create a new admin, delete logs, and exit without leaving a correlatable trail.

steps.action: Create a second Global Admin account as persistence.
steps.gain: Second admin account with no alert fired.

steps.action: Lower audit log retention or filter to hide the change.

title: Group owner can escalate by adding self to privileged group
narrative: A non-admin user is set as owner of a group that holds a privileged role via role assignment. Group owners can add members, including themselves. No IAM separation-of-duties control stops this.

steps.action: Use MS Graph PATCH /groups/<id>/members to add own user object.
Inherit the group's role; sign in to admin endpoints.

title: Dynamic group rule misconfig grants role to unintended users
narrative: A dynamic group rule (e.g. department = 'engineering') grants membership based on a user attribute. An attacker with User Admin or attribute-edit rights modifies a user's department to match, auto-joining them to the group — and inheriting whatever role the group holds.

steps.action: Edit a principal's department attribute to trigger the dynamic rule.
Sign in; role inherited via dynamic group.

title: On-prem sync admin compromises cloud
narrative: Entra Connect server holds a synchronization account that can write to the directory. The server is domain-joined and shares local admins with other workstations. Compromise the workstation → dump LSASS → own the sync account → write to Entra directly.

steps.action: Run Mimikatz to extract cached credentials of a shared local admin.
Impersonate sync account; write directly to Entra via MSOL account token.
steps.gain: Directory write access bypassing Conditional Access.

blast_radius.max_privilege: Directory write — equivalent to tenant admin.

title: Overprivileged device registration lets attacker bypass CA
narrative: Device registration is not restricted — any user in the tenant can register a new device. An attacker registers their attacker-controlled laptop as a 'compliant' device, then uses device-bound refresh tokens to satisfy the 'compliant device' Conditional Access requirement.

steps.action: Register their laptop as a tenant device.
Obtain PRT; use it to bypass CA policies that require compliant device.
steps.gain: Full session access to protected apps.

title: Application admin grants Graph app perms without review
narrative: A user holds Application Administrator (not Global Admin, but enough to grant admin consent for Graph application permissions). Combined with no review of consented apps, the admin can grant Directory.ReadWrite.All to a new app without detection and create a permanent backdoor.

steps.action: Register a new Entra app requesting Directory.ReadWrite.All as application permission.
Grant admin consent to the app (App Admin can).
steps.gain: Persistent tenant-wide directory write via app backdoor.

blast_radius.max_privilege: Tenant-wide directory write via planted app.

title: Certificate-based SP auth with long-lived cert
narrative: A service principal authenticates via a 10-year x.509 certificate. If the private key leaves the HSM once, there is no practical revocation. The SP holds Azure RBAC roles at subscription scope, making the leaked cert a decade-long backdoor.

steps.action: Exfiltrate the private key from a misconfigured Key Vault or developer laptop.
Authenticate to Azure AD using the certificate; persist access for the cert's remaining lifetime.

data_at_risk: Every resource the SP can reach

title: MFA fatigue against non-enforced user
narrative: A user has MFA configured but the Conditional Access policy is in report-only mode. An attacker with the password sprays MFA push notifications; when the policy is report-only, the attacker still gets the token even if the user denies the push (because the CA result was never enforced).

steps.action: Spray valid password + trigger dozens of push notifications.
steps.gain: Either user approves by mistake or CA-report-only lets it through.

steps.action: Token issued despite denied push because policy is report-only.
steps.gain: Authenticated session with full delegated scope.

data_at_risk: All apps the user can access

title: Privileged authentication admin can reset any MFA
narrative: The Privileged Authentication Administrator role is assigned to a user who does not need it. This role can reset MFA methods on any user — including Global Admins. A compromised holder can replace a Global Admin's MFA with attacker-controlled factors and then sign in as that admin.

steps.action: Identify a GA user in the directory.
Reset GA's MFA method to an attacker phone number.
Sign in as GA; password reset flow + attacker phone completes auth.

title: PIM active assignments used as normal role
narrative: PIM roles are assigned as Active (not Eligible) so they never expire or require activation. This defeats the purpose of PIM — a compromised principal has permanent admin, not just-in-time admin.

steps.action: Sign in; role already active, no activation needed.
Operate freely; no activation log to correlate on.

title: Access review disabled or never runs
narrative: No access review is configured for privileged groups AND users with roles are never required to re-justify their access. Role assignments accumulate as people change teams. Eventually the tenant has 30+ Global Admins nobody can justify.

steps.action: Users change jobs but keep old roles because nobody reviews.
Target any one of the many admins who no longer need the role.

title: Group with external owners creates persistence
narrative: A Microsoft 365 / Entra group has an external (guest) user listed as owner. Guest owners can edit membership and — depending on group settings — modify role assignments. A partner breach turns into a home tenant persistence mechanism.

steps.action: Compromise the guest owner account on the partner side.
Add attacker-controlled user object to the group; role inherits.

data_at_risk: Home tenant directory objects reachable via the group's role

title: Stale service principal with no owner
narrative: A service principal exists with no listed owner AND a never-expiring credential. The original developer left years ago. The SP still has Contributor on a production subscription. No one will notice if its credential is used.

steps.action: Discover the SP's client secret in an old CI log or GitHub history.
Use SP to operate on prod; no owner gets notified of anomalous activity.

services_at_risk: Whatever the stale SP can touch

title: Guest user can invite more guests — supply-chain invitation
narrative: Guest users are allowed to invite other guests (Restrict Guest Access setting is lax). Once one partner user is compromised, the attacker invites attacker-controlled identities into the home tenant and walks up the role graph from there.

steps.action: Invite attacker@evil.com as guest.
Apply normal escalation chains (CHAIN-052, CHAIN-072).

title: Cross-tenant access without inbound trust restrictions
narrative: Cross-tenant access settings allow any partner to authenticate into apps with inbound MFA/device claims trust — but the home tenant hasn't scoped which partner tenants qualify. Any tenant admin with a hostile user can auth to home apps with partner-claimed MFA.

steps.action: Set up a tenant that claims MFA for all sign-ins.
Cross-tenant sign-in to home app; MFA claim satisfied externally.
steps.gain: Authenticated on home app with fake MFA assurance.

blast_radius.max_privilege: Whatever the app grants to guests.
services_at_risk: Every app published to the tenant

title: Federated identity provider trust compromise
narrative: Tenant federates authentication to a third-party IdP (ADFS, Okta, Ping). If the IdP's signing cert is stolen or the IdP itself is compromised, the attacker can mint SAML tokens claiming any UPN — Global Admin included. This is the Solorigate pattern.

steps.action: Exfiltrate the SAML token-signing private key.
steps.gain: Ability to forge any SAML assertion.

steps.action: Mint a SAML token claiming the UPN of a Global Admin; present to Entra.
steps.gain: Forged Global Admin session, no password needed.
